info.name: Jadal — Admin Platform Statistics
info.description:
Admin-only platform-wide statistics (5 stats × JSON + Excel export) plus the updated complaint submission endpoint.

Setup: set `base_url` (no trailing slash) and `admin_token` (Sanctum token of a user with role=admin) in the collection variables. All stats endpoints return 403 for non-admins.

JSON envelope: `{ success, message, data }`. Export endpoints return an .xlsx download — in Postman use **Send and Download** (arrow next to Send).

Responses are server-cached ~10 minutes per endpoint+filter combination.

Full reference: ADMIN_STATS_API_DOCS.md in the repo.

query.description: Default 5. Below this a framework is never flagged (still listed).
YYYY-MM inclusive lower bound on debates.scheduled_at
YYYY-MM inclusive upper bound (must be >= from)
CSV of debate_formats.id

request.description:
Detects frameworks whose motions structurally favor one side.

imbalance_score = |prop_win_rate − 0.5| × 2 (0 = fair, 1 = one-sided). flagged = imbalance > 0.3 AND n_debates >= min_n_debates. Draws count in n_debates but are a win for neither side.

A debate counts once per framework its motion carries (many-to-many fan-out) — summing n_debates across frameworks may exceed the platform total. Sorted flagged-first, then imbalance desc.

Note: group_by is NOT accepted here (no time dimension in the response).

query.description: Same params as the JSON endpoint

request.description: Same filters as the JSON endpoint; returns jadal-framework-fairness-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.xlsx. Flagged rows come pre-highlighted. Use Send and Download.

query.description: REQUIRED: most_improved | win_rate | avg_score | most_active
Default 10, max 50
Default 3 — debaters below this are excluded entirely

request.description:
Runs the SAME Improvement Index math as the mobile per-debater endpoint (same buckets/regression/weights/bands) for every debater, then ranks.

value = improvement index; band = strong_upward | improving | stable | regressing | sharp_decline (band only on this board).

This board excludes draws and additionally requires >= 3 distinct active months (or years on long spans) — debaters without a computable trend are omitted.
value = wins / completed debates as debater (0..1 fraction). Draws count in the denominator, no band field.
value = average speech score on the platform's 0..100 scale (all of the debater's scored stages, draws included).
value = integer count of completed debates as debater in the window.

query.description: REQUIRED — the board name is baked into the sheet + filename
none (default, single 'all' bucket) | year | month
Strongly recommended with group_by=month — unbounded month series over >24 months history returns 422
none | role (adds by_role) | debate_format (adds by_format per bucket)
CSV of debate_formats.id — filters the debate metrics

request.description:
The admin homepage pulse-check: new users by role, debates created/completed/cancelled (+ cancellation_breakdown), completion_rate, avg_debates_per_active_debater — per time bucket.

Dating: new_users & debates_created by created_at; completed/cancelled/participation by scheduled_at — so completion_rate CAN exceed 1 in a bucket (don't clamp). Ratios are null when the denominator is 0. Empty buckets are omitted.

422 month guard: group_by=month without explicit from+to is rejected when platform history spans more than 24 months.

query.description: debate_format adds indented per-format sub-rows under each bucket

request.description: Returns jadal-platform-health-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.xlsx (one row per bucket). Use Send and Download.

query.description: all (default) | churn_risk | ramping_up
Default 1
Default 25, max 100
Default 30 — the 'recent activity' window
Default 90 — baseline window immediately before the recent one
Default 30 — quiet longer than this ⇒ churn candidate

request.description:
Every debater with >=1 completed debate, classified churn_risk / ramping_up / stable. Rolling day-windows anchored to now — the universal from/to/group_by/formats filters do NOT apply here.

churn_risk = quiet > threshold AND had baseline activity; ramping_up = recent per-day rate > 1.5× baseline per-day rate (any recent activity with a zero baseline counts); else stable.

Paginated (meta.page/per_page/total), ordered most-urgent-first (churn risks, longest-quiet first).

query.description: Applies to the export too

request.description: Contains EVERY matching row — page/per_page are ignored on the export. churn_risk rows pre-highlighted. Returns jadal-engagement-churn-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.xlsx. Use Send and Download.

query.description: Default 3 — entries with less exposure are hidden
debater | trainer | judge | chair
open | under_review | resolved | dismissed
Bounds complaint created_at AND the exposure window (scheduled_at)
Applies to the exposure denominator only

request.description:
complaints_per_100_debates = complaints against the user / completed debates where they held that capacity × 100. One entry per (user, capacity) pair; 'chair' = judge rows with is_chair=true.

unattributed_total = legacy complaints with no target recorded (excluded from per-person figures).

avg_time_to_last_update_hours_approx is an APPROXIMATION (updated_at − created_at on currently resolved/dismissed rows — no resolved_at column exists). Sorted by the per-100 rate desc.
Same filters as the JSON endpoint; the unattributed count is appended at the bottom of the sheet. Returns jadal-complaint-accountability-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.xlsx. Use Send and Download.

Existing endpoint, any authenticated user (not admin-only — swap the token if testing as a debater).

NEW optional fields: target_user_id (must exist in users) + target_role (debater | trainer | judge | chair). They are a PAIR — sending one without the other returns 422. Omitting both keeps legacy behavior, but such complaints only ever appear in unattributed_total on the accountability dashboard.

The response (ComplaintResource) now includes target_user_id, target_role, and target_user (null on legacy rows).

item.name: File complaint WITHOUT target (legacy shape, still valid)

request.description: Pre-existing request shape — unchanged and still works (201). Stored with target_user_id/target_role = null → counted only in unattributed_total on the accountability stat.
